subject: Chemistry
q: The structure of Tagamet (Cimetidine) is:
question: The structure of Tagamet (Cimetidine) is:
solution: Cimetidine is a member of the class of guanidines that consists of guanidine carrying a methyl substituent at position 1, a cyano group at position 2 and a 2-{[(5-methyl-1H-imidazol-4-yl)methyl]sulfanyl}ethyl group at position 3.

I. They activate many enzymes.

solution:
I. They activate many enzymes.
II. They participate in the oxidation of glucose to produce ATP.
III. Along with sodium ions, they are responsible for the transmission of nerve signals.
All are correct.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following compounds are not used as disinfectants?

A. Chloroxylenol
B. Bithional
C. Veronal
D. Prontosil
E. Terpineol

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Which of the following compounds are not used as disinfectants?

A. Chloroxylenol
B. Bithional
C. Veronal
D. Prontosil
E. Terpineol

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Antiseptics and disinfectants are also chemicals that either kill or prevent the growth of microorganisms. Antiseptics are applied to living tissues such as wounds, cuts, ulcers and diseased skin surfaces. Examples are furacine, soframicine, etc. Commonly used antiseptic, Dettol is a mixture of chloroxylenol and terpineol. Bithionol (the compound is also called bithional) is added to soaps to impart antiseptic properties. Protonsil is an antibiotic, whereas veronal is a tranquilizer which is used for the treatment of stress and mild or severe mental diseases.

So, Veronal and Prontosil are not used as disinfectant.

subject: Chemistry
q: Drugs used to bind to receptors, inhibiting its natural function and blocking a message are called
question: Drugs used to bind to receptors, inhibiting its natural function and blocking a message are called
solution: Drugs that bind to receptor site and inhibit the natural functions are known as antagonists. Agonist are type of drugs that activate the natural messenger by switching on the receptor, useful when lack of chemical messenger. If the drug binds to a site other than active site in an enzyme, it is known as allosteric site.

  List-I
Enzyme		   List-II
Conversion of
A. Invertase I. Starch into maltose
B. Zymase II. Maltose into glucose
C. Diastase III. Glucose into ethanol
D. Maltase IV. Cane sugar into glucose

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Match List I with List II.

  List-I
Enzyme		   List-II
Conversion of
A. Invertase I. Starch into maltose
B. Zymase II. Maltose into glucose
C. Diastase III. Glucose into ethanol
D. Maltase IV. Cane sugar into glucose

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
solution:
Invertase helps in the conversion of sucrose to glucose and fructose.

Zymase catalyses the conversion of glucose or fructose to ethyl alcohol.

Diastase is a starch hydrolysing enzyme that breaks down the complex carbohydrate (polysaccharides, i.e. starch) into simple carbohydrates (monosaccharides, i.e. simple sugar like glucose).

Maltase catalyses the conversion of maltose to glucose.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following compounds is an example of hypnotic drug?
question: Which of the following compounds is an example of hypnotic drug?
solution:
Derivatives of barbituric acid viz., veronal, amytal, nembutal, luminal and seconal constitute an important class of tranquilizers. These derivatives are called barbiturates. Barbiturates are hypnotic. i.e., sleep producing agents.

Amytal is hypnotic drug used to treat sleep disorder.

subject: Chemistry
q: The reaction used for preparation of soap from fat is :
question: The reaction used for preparation of soap from fat is :
solution: The reaction used for preparation of soap from fat is not alkaline hydrolysis, but rather saponification. In saponification, a triglyceride (fat or oil) is reacted with a strong base, such as sodium hydroxide (or potassium hydroxide, in the presence of heat to produce soap molecules and glycerol as a byproduct. The reaction involves the hydrolysis of the ester bond in the triglyceride by the base to form carboxylate ions (the soap molecules) and glycerol.

subject: Chemistry
q: The reason for "drug induced poisoning" is:
question: The reason for "drug induced poisoning" is:
options: binding irreversibly to the active site of the enzyme.
binding at the allosteric sites of the enzyme.
binding reversibly at the active site of the enzyme.
brining conformational change in the binding site of enzyme.
solution: The reason for drug induced poisoning is the binding of drug, irreversibly, to the active site of the enzyme. If this happens, then body degenerates the enzyme and releases a new enzyme.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following artificial sweeteners has the highest sweetness value in comparison to cane sugar?
question: Which of the following artificial sweeteners has the highest sweetness value in comparison to cane sugar?
solution:
Saccharin, also called ortho-sulphobenzimide, is the first popular artificial sweetening agent. It is about 550 times as sweet as cane sugar. Its use is of great value to diabetic persons and people who need to control their intake of calories.

Aspartame is the most successful and widely used artificial sweetener. It is roughly 100 times as sweet as cane sugar. It is the methyl ester of dipeptide formed from aspartic acid and phenylalanine. Use of aspartame is limited to cold foods and soft drinks because it is unstable at cooking temperature.

Alitame is a high-potency sweetener, and it is roughly 2000 times as sweet as cane sugar.

Sucralose is trichloro derivative of sucrose. Its appearance and taste are like sugar. It is roughly 600 times as sweet as cane sugar.

So, sweetness value order with respect to cane sugar is:

Alitame > Sucralose > Saccharin > Aspartame

question: Among the following organic acids, the acid present in rancid butter is :
solution: Butyric acid is a fatty acid occuring in the form of esters in animal fats when butter goes rancid, butyric acid is liberated from the glyceride by hydrolysis, leading to unpleasant odor.

solution: Norethindrone is an example of synthetic progesterone derivative most widely used as antifertility drug. Meprobamate, is relatively mild tranquilizer suitable for relieving tension. Synthetic drugs, brompheniramine (Dimetapp) and terfenadine (Seldane), act as antihistamines. Ampicillin and Amoxycillin are synthetic modifications of penicillins. subject: Chemistry
q: Stearic acid and polyethylene glycol react to form which one of the following soap/s detergents?
question: Stearic acid and polyethylene glycol react to form which one of the following soap/s detergents?
solution:
Non-ionic detergents do not contain any ion in their constitution. One such detergent is formed when stearic acid reacts with polyethylene glycol.

CH3CH216COOHStearic acid+HOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OHPolyethyleneglycol →-H2OCH3CH216COOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OH

 A type of detergent in which the active part of the molecule is a positive ion (cation). Cationic detergents are usually quaternary ammonium salts.

Anionic detergent is a synthetic detergent in which a lipophilic hydrocarbon group of the molecule is an anion. A detergent molecule consists of a long hydrocarbon chain and a water-soluble negative ionic group.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List - I with List - II.

  List-I   List-II
A I Dishwashing powder
B II Toothpaste
C C17H35COO-Na++Na2CO3+ Rosinate III Laundry soap
D CH3CH216COOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OH IV Hair conditioner

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List - I with List - II.

  List-I   List-II
A I Dishwashing powder
B II Toothpaste
C C17H35COO-Na++Na2CO3+ Rosinate III Laundry soap
D CH3CH216COOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OH IV Hair conditioner

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
1. Laundry soaps contain fillers like sodium rosinate, sodium silicate, borax and sodium carbonate.

2. Cationic detergents are quaternary ammonium salts of amines with acetates, chlorides or bromides as anions. Cationic part possess a long hydrocarbon chain and a positive charge on nitrogen atom. Hence, these are called cationic detergents. Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide is a popular cationic detergent and is used in hair conditioners.

3. Non-ionic Detergents: Non-ionic detergents do not contain any ion in their constitution. One such detergent is formed when stearic acid reacts with polyethyleneglycol. Liquid dishwashing detergents are non-ionic type.

4. In anionic detergents, the anionic part of the molecule is involved in the cleansing action. Sodium salts of alkylbenzenesulphonates are an important class of anionic detergents. They are mostly used for household work.

    (e) Analgesic
question:
Match the following drugs with their therapeutic actions: 

(i) Ranitidine (a) Antidepressant
(ii) Nardil (Phenelzine) (b) Antibiotic
(iii) Chloramphenicol (c) Antihistamine
(iv) Dimetane (Brompheniramine) (d) Antacid
    (e) Analgesic
solution: We know that drugs help us diagnose and cure numerous illnesses. But any drug consumed by humans can actually have two kinds of effects - therapeutic effects and side effects. The goal is to prescribe the ideal drug, the one that only has a therapeutic effect.

subject: Chemistry
q:
A. Ammonium salts produce haze in atmosphere.

B. Ozone gets produced when atmospheric oxygen reacts with chlorine radicals.

C. Polychlorinated biphenyls act as cleansing solvents.

D. ‘Blue baby’ syndrome occurs due to the presence of excess of sulphate ions in water.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:-
question:
A. Ammonium salts produce haze in atmosphere.

B. Ozone gets produced when atmospheric oxygen reacts with chlorine radicals.

C. Polychlorinated biphenyls act as cleansing solvents.

D. ‘Blue baby’ syndrome occurs due to the presence of excess of sulphate ions in water.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:-
solution:
A. Ammonium salts produce atmospheric haze (aerosol of fine particles).

B. Ozone is produced by 3 atoms of oxygen. Some of the molecules in the atmosphere break due to sunlight and oxygen atoms are released. The oxygen atoms then react with other oxygen molecules to form ozone. The overall reaction is 

O2 →UV O + O

O + O2 → O3

C. Polychlorinated biphenyls have the number of applications including their use as cleansing solvents. 

D. 'Blue baby' syndrome occurs due to the presence of excess nitrate ions and not sulphate ions in water.

From the above statement we concluded that Statement A and C are correct.

solution: Enzymes are very specific in nature.

subject: Chemistry
q: The artificial sweetener that has the highest sweetness value in comparison to cane sugar is:
question: The artificial sweetener that has the highest sweetness value in comparison to cane sugar is:
solution: Natural sweeteners, e.g., sucrose add to calorie intake and therefore many people prefer to use artificial sweeteners. Ortho-sulphobenzimide, also called saccharin, is the first popular artificial sweetening agent. It is about 550 times as sweet as cane sugar. Aspartame is 100 times as sweet as cane sugar. Sucralose is 600 times as sweet as cane sugar. Alitame is an artificial sweetener that has 1000 times the sweetness value of cane sugar.

subject: Chemistry
q: The following molecule acts as an :
question: The following molecule acts as an :
solution:
Synthetic drugs, brompheniramine (Dimetapp) act as antihistamines.

Antihistamines are drugs which treat hay fever and other allergies. Typically, people take antihistamines as an inexpensive, not patented, drug that can be bought without a prescription and relieves from nasal congestion, sneezing, or hives caused by pollen, dust mites, or animal allergy with few side effects.

We know that drugs help us diagnose and cure numerous illnesses. But any drug consumed by humans can actually have two kinds of effects - therapeutic effects and side effects. The goal is to prescribe the ideal drug, the one that only has a therapeutic effect.

subject: Chemistry
q: Using very little soap while washing clothes, does not serve the purpose of cleaning of clothes, because
question: Using very little soap while washing clothes, does not serve the purpose of cleaning of clothes, because
options: soap particles remain floating in water as ions.
colloidal structure of soap in water is completely disturbed.
the hydrophobic part of soap is not able to take away grease.
the micelles are not formed due to concentration of soap, below its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CMC</mi></math> value.
solution: Micelle or associate colloid formation occurs above a certain concentration known as CMC. So, if the concentration of soap is less that means it hasn't reached the CMC value. That is why it does not serve the purpose of cleaning of clothes.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following is not a broad spectrum antibiotic?
question: Which of the following is not a broad spectrum antibiotic?
solution:
A broad-spectrum antibiotic is an antibiotic that acts on the two major bacterial groups, gram-positive and gram-negative, or any antibiotic that acts against a wide range of disease-causing bacteria.

Penicillin G has a narrow spectrum. Ampicillin and Amoxycillin are synthetic modifications of penicillins. These have broad spectrum. 

Chloramphenicol is a broad spectrum antibiotic. It is rapidly absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract and hence can be given orally in case of typhoid, dysentery, acute fever, certain form of urinary infections, meningitis and pneumonia.
